title: pulsatilla × magyarica
intro: Pulsatilla × magyarica, also known as the Hungarian pasque flower, is a rare and hybrid plant that belongs to the genus Pulsatilla and is native to Hungary. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to induce sweating and relieve respiratory issues. The health benefits of Pulsatilla × magyarica include its potential to alleviate symptoms of the common cold, coughs, and bronchitis, as well as its use as a diuretic to reduce fluid retention. In herbal preparations, Pulsatilla × magyarica is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
